boundary_violations: Support/Agent admission boundary weakness: the Agent resource resolver path resolves tool_policy_resources from agent/ and would reject unknown policy files, but AgentAdapterRequest itself accepts arbitrary tool_policy_refs and the support read-tier predicate admits by intersection. This does not choose Link Movement or judge quality, but it weakens fail-closed admission at the adapter surface.
checked_work: Uncommitted tracked diff vs HEAD for lane tool-policy three-tier repair.
Read-tier admission, write-tier precedence, Gemini none-tier documentation, artifact-grounding comparison/gate propagation, chat-session park exclusion, and checker probes.
missing_or_mismatched_facts: Mismatch against the stated closed-tier claim: agent_request_read_tier uses intersection with READ_ONLY_TOOL_POLICY_REFS, so a mixed unknown-plus-admitted policy request reaches read instead of none.
Checker gap: _agent_read_tier_probe covers reviewer-readonly positive, leader-coordination positive, read-write-only none, and Gemini none, but does not cover unknown-plus-admitted ambiguity.
narrowly_proven: The current diff compiles and has no whitespace diff-check failures under the commands run.
The current direct agent_adapter_return_shape kernel function passes and inspects read-tier and artifact-grounding behavior.
Current active Agent Objects, when read directly from agent/objects/*.yaml, produce the expected nominal read/write/none tier table.
The ambiguity edge for a direct adapter request with unknown-plus-reviewer policy is reproducible in the current worktree.
not_proven: Full check_profile.py --all was not run because this read-only sandbox has no usable temporary directory.
The profile-level FIRE mutations were not executed; only current-code probes and checker-condition inspection were observed.
Live provider behavior, provider quality, Codex native sync, Claude native sync, MCP projection behavior.
Source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, and Movement authority.
observed_evidence: BROKEN: one adversarial edge does not hold. A direct AgentAdapterRequest with tool_policy_refs=("tool-policy:unknown", "tool-policy:reviewer-readonly") entered the read tier and rendered read-only inspection rules instead of failing closed to none.

Direct probe output for that edge: unknown-only -> read false and no-tools; unknown-plus-reviewer -> read true with read-only repository inspection rules; read-write-only -> read false; leader-plus-readwrite -> read true.

Full lane sweep over the 8 active Agent Objects observed expected nominal behavior: coo/pm-lead/design-lead/cto-lead/qa-lead/qa/inspector non-write requests enter read on codex-local and claude-local; gemini-local and chat-session stay none; dev non-write stays none. Write-scope requests for pm-lead/design-lead/cto-lead/dev/qa-lead on codex-local or claude-local enter write, not read.

Artifact-grounding trigger probe observed axis-attack-qa, code-attack-qa, evidence-integrity, and review trigger evidence_used grounding; design triggers evidence_refs grounding; closure triggers no grounding field.

Commands observed: git diff --check exit 0; compileall over changed Python files exit 0; direct run_agent_adapter_return_shape inspected 31 and passed; building_plans_boundary_sweep inspected 4 and passed.
Profile runner command for building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening was blocked by this read-only environment: Python reported no usable temporary directory.
observed_matches: Write tier precedence is preserved: _build_prompt checks agent_request_effective_write before agent_request_read_tier, and _codex_sandbox_for_request maps non-effective-write to read-only.
Claude read tier renders plan mode with Read,Grep,Glob only; Gemini with an admitted read policy still receives a documented none-tier limit and no-tools rule.
Packet-only code/design returns are covered by the direct artifact-grounding checker probe; missing repository artifact refs become missing comparison fields and Link gate missing_required_facts.
Closure return shape lacks the trigger pairs checked by _artifact_grounding_evidence_field, so closure is not caught by the new grounding requirement in the direct trigger probe.
Chat-session is excluded from read-tier by adapter_ref filter and connect_agent_brain still parks adapter:chat-session before local CLI invocation.

deferred_smith_review_queue: Decide whether direct AgentAdapterRequest helper semantics are in-scope for the claimed closed tier decision.
narrowly_proven: The direct helper-level ambiguous/unknown policy case does not fail closed to none.
No inspected read-tier prompt path leaked edit/write/git permission wording.
Effective write remains gated by write_scope + read-write-scoped policy + observed-write adapter in the smoke run.
Packet-only artifact grounding is covered by in-process checker probes for code-attack-qa and design.

not_proven: Full profile green under this sandbox.
chat_session_park_seam live checker green, because it also requires a temp directory.
Complete absence of future support-authority leaks.
Whether direct AgentAdapterRequest construction is an admitted public API boundary or only an internal helper surface.
observed_evidence: Finding: direct AgentAdapterRequest construction with tool_policy_refs=(tool-policy:reviewer-readonly, tool-policy:unknown) still enters read tier. Repro command returned True/read-only/True for agent_request_read_tier, codex sandbox, and read-only prompt rule presence. Evidence: support/connection/agent_adapter.py:525 defines read tier, and lines 536-540 only require non-write plus intersection with READ_ONLY_TOOL_POLICY_REFS plus codex/claude adapter; AgentAdapterRequest __post_init__ at support/connection/agent_adapter.py:281-330 does not validate tool_policy_refs.

Normal run path is narrower than the direct helper gap: run builds request tool_policy_refs from prepared.agent_object.tool_policy_refs at support/operator/run.py:1877-1920, and file-backed Agent Objects pass through validate_agent_refs at support/operator/run.py:1681-1721; unknown Agent resource refs resolve through agent/tool_policies paths at support/connection/agent_resources.py:333-370.

No catastrophic read-tier write leak observed in inspected code. Read-tier prompt rules explicitly allow read/search/diff/checker commands and forbid edit/create/delete/write plus git mutations at support/connection/agent_adapter.py:1138-1149; Claude read tier returns plan mode with Read,Grep,Glob only at support/connection/agent_adapter.py:1041-1046.

Write tier behavior smoke held: direct effective-write probe returned True/workspace-write only with write_scope + tool-policy:read-write-scoped; dev no-write request returned False read-tier and read-only sandbox. Code gate is support/connection/agent_adapter.py:513-522.

Gemini stays none in the read-tier decision because agent_request_read_tier only admits codex-local and claude-local at support/connection/agent_adapter.py:540; prompt documents gemini none-tier at support/connection/agent_adapter.py:1153-1162.

Artifact-grounding logic is present: review/design grounding facts are declared at support/operator/plan_validation.py:78-87, added to comparison required/missing evidence at support/operator/plan_validation.py:596-641, and triggered for attacked_work/attacked_scope/evidence_scope/checked_work/design_summary shapes at support/operator/plan_validation.py:644-656.
Artifact-grounding propagates to gates: support/operator/run.py:1545-1574 and support/operator/gate_sequence.py:80-83,250-255 append .repository_artifact_ref fields into gate base required fields.

Brick contracts now require repository artifact refs: review at brick/templates/bricks/review/brick.md:24-29 and design at brick/templates/bricks/design/brick.md:24-26; analogous changes exist in code-attack-qa, axis-attack-qa, and evidence-integrity.
Checker kernel direct call passed: run_agent_adapter_return_shape inspected 31 targets; direct _agent_read_tier_probe returned 10 and _artifact_grounding_probe returned 4.
Full profile command was blocked by read-only sandbox tempdir failure: check_profile.py --profile building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening exited 1 with 'No usable temporary directory found'.
